title: Free Yoga Practice
description:
Iama Embodied Community
🌀 **Move with intention. Connect with your body. Celebrate community.**

Join us every **Thursday at 6 PM** for an embodied yoga practice designed to help you release tension, ground yourself, and cultivate presence. This is a trauma-informed, all-levels class, no prior yoga experience needed. Expect a Hatha-Vinyasa flow, breathwork, and a space to listen to your body’s wisdom.

🌿 **Every last Thursday of the month, stay after class for a community potluck dinner!**

📍 **Location**: 727 T St. Sacramento CA 95811
✨ **All our events are free. Donations are welcome.**
📩 **Questions? Reach out at** embody.iama@gmail.com
🔗 **Follow us on Instagram:** [@embody.iama](https://www.instagram.com/embody.iama)

title: JOURNEY THROUGH THE REDWOODS
description:
Iama Embodied Community
Tucked away in the Oakland Hills, this hidden gem is home to mostly second- and third-generation coastal redwoods, descendants of
the ancient giants that once stood alongside the sacred California grizzly and soaring condors. Today, these trees stretch skyward, carrying that legacy forward.

This will be an easy to moderate 3-mile journey, with approximately 350 feet of cumulative elevation gain. Along the way, we’ll encounter a variety of wild berries, from strawberries and huckleberries to the soft, velvety touch of thimbleberries and more.

Michael will share practical knowledge on
how to locate water in the landscape, and reading subtle signs from birds, trees, and leaves.
You’ll also learn how to recognize when you’re nearing the ocean, guided by the presence of specific plant species.

Elizabeth will offer insights into crows and ravens, marveling at their deep sense of community, reverence, and the way they invite us to
see the world with curiosity and wonder.
We’ll finish the journey with a relaxed tea gathering. Hot water will be provided; please bring your favorite tea and a snack to share.

Exact Address: Stream Trail, Castro Valley CA 94552, United States

title: Art in Action: Spectacular Seed Bombs
description:
Pence Family Art Workshops
Learn about the ancient Japanese practice of seed bombs! Using air-dry clay and native wildflower seeds, make seed bombs that you can later plant outdoors or in a garden. Decorate your seed bombs with dried petals and stamps, and design and illustrate a plant care card to help you take care of the wildflowers you plant.

Art in Action: Spectacular Seed Bombs
Saturday, May 30, 1-3 PM
$5 Members, $10 Non-Members (per person, all materials included)

**How it works:**
Art in Action engages kids in art projects that are fun, creative, and collaborative with their family members. This is a drop-in program for ages 5-12. No registration in advance is required, but space and supplies are limited to the first 25 participants and are available on a first come, first served basis.

When you arrive, pay with cash or card at the front desk. A parent or adult caregiver is required to stay, but only has to pay for the project for themselves if they are also doing art. Stay as long or as short as you like over the two hour period.

Become a Member starting at $70/year and save! If you join the Pence Gallery when you attend this workshop, you can enjoy Member pricing for Art in Action, plus receive a $5 gift certificate to our gift shop as a thank you. Membership supports art for all ages and makes creative moments like this possible—jump in and be part of it.

We have a different Art in Action family workshop every month! Learn more at [https://pencegallery.org/art-in-action/](https://pencegallery.org/art-in-action/).

This unique monthly program is generously sponsored and made possible by Mary Stephens DeWall and Les DeWall.

title: Mosaics Acoustic Guitar & Singing Group
description:
Davis Art, Music & Outdoor Activities Meetup
**Discover Intercultural Mosaics This Winter 2025–2026!**

Join us for a vibrant and uplifting season of virtual learning and community from **December 20, 2025 to March 28, 2026**—all from the comfort of your home on Zoom!

**Registration is required, and the Zoom link will be provided exclusively to those who sign up.**

**Bi-Weekly Friday Zoom Sessions** *(Every 2nd and 4th Friday)*
• **4:00 PM** – Advanced Korean with Stephen
• **5:00 PM** – Intermediate Conversational Chinese with Sam
• **6:00 PM** – Bible Study & Meditation Group with Stephen
• **7:00 PM** – Beginning Chinese with Jane

**Weekly Saturday Zoom Sessions** (total of 12 sessions)
• **4:00 PM** – Beginning Spanish with Viviana
• **5:00 PM** – Beginning to Intermediate Korean with Stephen
• **6:00 PM** – Beginning to Intermediate Japanese with Norio
• **7:00 PM** – English Learners Group with Linda & Chad

**Important Notes:**

1. **No Zoom sessions** on **January 2–3, 2026** (New Year) and **April 3–4, 2026** (Easter).
2. **Special Gatherings (Tentative):**
**• February 14, 2026:** Valentine’s Day & pre–Lunar New Year celebration in person at Phoenix Hall, 2nd Floor, Davis Community Church.
**• March 14, 2026:** Additional in-person gathering (tentative).

**Return to In-Person Meetings:**
We will resume our **regular in-person gatherings on April 11, 2026** at the **2nd floor rooms of Phoenix Hall, Davis Community Church**.

For more information about Intercultural Mosaics classes and activities, visit **[www.nextg.org](http://www.nextg.org)** or contact **Dr. Stephen Moon** at **916-217-5470** or **catalyst@nextg.org**.

title: Botanical Blueprints with Linda Clark Johnson
description:
Pence Adult Art Programs
You don’t need to be an artist to enjoy making beautiful blue cyanotypes! In this introductory class with artist Linda Clark Johnson, you’ll learn all the basics of this fun and easy alternative photographic technique. Using light-sensitive chemicals, you’ll expose prints of plants in the sun and process them in water. By the time you leave, you’ll have 4 gorgeous 8 x 10” blueprints and the know-how to create more at home.

Botanical Blueprints with Linda Clark Johnson
Sunday, May 31, 10 AM - 2 PM
$115 Members \| $125 Non\-Members
(All materials included except for flowers and foliage, which participants should bring)

**Registration is required through the [Pence's website (CLICK HERE)](https://pencegallery.org/education/programs/workshops-and-classes/).**

Any questions? Email pencegallery@gmail.com.

title: Blood on the Clocktower at Blue Note
description:
Yolo County Board Game Gathering
The first Wednesday of every month, we play _Blood on the Clocktower_, a social deduction game in the vein of Werewolf/Mafia/The Resistance, with a few twists!

We'll have a circle of chairs reserved inside Blue Note, near the back, with a Yolo County Board Game Gathering sign on the table. Just say hello, and we'll show you some games, pull some players together, and get to having fun!

We also usually have a few tables of players playing other board games, if social deduction isn't your bag!

Get a feel for Blood on the Clocktower here: https://youtu.be/4sfa8_kNxsQ
source: Meetup Yolo County Board Game Gathering

title: Art in Action: Creative Sun Print Collages
description:
Pence Family Art Workshops
Create beautiful blue sun prints of plants and flowers! Using special blue photo paper, the sun’s rays, and some water, create amazing prints (known as cyanotypes) of leaves, flower petals, and more. Transform these prints into one-of-a-kind collages through cutting, layering, and adding natural materials and other found objects.

Art in Action: Creative Sun Print Collages
Sunday, June 7, 1-3 PM
$5 Members, $10 Non-Members (per person, all materials included)

**How it works:**
Art in Action engages kids in art projects that are fun, creative, and collaborative with their family members. This is a drop-in program for ages 5-12. No registration in advance is required, but space and supplies are limited to the first 25 participants and are available on a first come, first served basis.

When you arrive, pay with cash or card at the front desk. A parent or adult caregiver is required to stay, but only has to pay for the project for themselves if they are also doing art. Stay as long or as short as you like over the two hour period.

Become a Member starting at $70/year and save! If you join the Pence Gallery when you attend this workshop, you can enjoy Member pricing for Art in Action, plus receive a $5 gift certificate to our gift shop as a thank you. Membership supports art for all ages and makes creative moments like this possible—jump in and be part of it.

We have a different Art in Action family workshop every month! Learn more at [https://pencegallery.org/art-in-action/](https://pencegallery.org/art-in-action/).

This unique monthly program is generously sponsored and made possible by Mary Stephens DeWall and Les DeWall.

title: Weekly Game Night at Blue Note
description:
Yolo County Board Game Gathering
Update 8/14/25:
**We're still active**, but we primarily use Discord to chat and plan events. (See group description for invite link.)

We still meet every Wednesday for board games at Blue Note Brewing Company. We'll have a handful of tables reserved near the back; just look for the pile of board games.

People usually start arriving around 5pm, and games start whenever people are ready. Blue Note closes at 9pm, and although we have a 5-10 minute grace period there, you should be in the process of final scoring by 9, or else pack it up and play again another day.

Game night is open to all ages! Blue Note is a brewery, not a bar, so there's no restriction against minors. In addition to their crafted-in-house beer selection, water and sodas are available.

You're not required to buy anything in order to join us, but we do encourage you to support local businesses. Blue Note's chef team makes a mean pizza, and there are other nearby downtown restaurants and businesses.
source: Meetup Yolo County Board Game Gathering

• **5:00 PM** – Beginning to Intermediate Korean with Stephen
• **6:00 PM** – Beginning to Intermediate Japanese with Norio
• **7:00 PM** – English Learners Group with Linda & Chad

**Important Notes:**

1. **No Zoom sessions** on **January 2–3, 2026** (New Year) and **April 3–4, 2026** (Easter).
2. **Special Gatherings (Tentative):**
**• February 14, 2026:** Valentine’s Day & pre–Lunar New Year celebration in person at Phoenix Hall, 2nd Floor, Davis Community Church.
**• March 14, 2026:** Additional in-person gathering (tentative).

**Return to In-Person Meetings:**
We will resume our **regular in-person gatherings on April 11, 2026** at the **2nd floor rooms of Phoenix Hall, Davis Community Church**.

For more information about Intercultural Mosaics classes and activities, visit **[www.nextg.org](http://www.nextg.org)** or contact **Dr. Stephen Moon** at **916-217-5470** or **catalyst@nextg.org**.

title: Intermediate Watercolor Painting with Misuk Goltz (6-Sessions)
description:
Pence Adult Art Programs
In this 6-week course, artist Misuk Goltz will guide you through essential watercolor techniques to help you paint your favorite subjects more expressively. Sessions will cover choosing inspiring subjects and methods for creating colors, textures, and values to elevate your art. Each class includes a critique of participants' completed work, live demonstrations, and extended individual guidance. Please note that this class is intended for participants who already have some watercolor painting experience.

Intermediate Watercolor Painting with Misuk Goltz
Tuesdays\, 10 AM \- 12 PM \| Jun\. 23\, 30 & Jul\. 7\, 14\, 21\, 28
$195 Members \| $215 Non\-Members \(Materials not included\)

**Registration is required through the [Pence's website (CLICK HERE)](https://pencegallery.org/education/programs/workshops-and-classes/).**

Any questions? Email pencegallery@gmail.com.

title: Weekly Game Night at Blue Note
description:
Yolo County Board Game Gathering
Update 8/14/25:
**We're still active**, but we primarily use Discord to chat and plan events. (See group description for invite link.)

We still meet every Wednesday for board games at Blue Note Brewing Company. We'll have a handful of tables reserved near the back; just look for the pile of board games.

People usually start arriving around 5pm, and games start whenever people are ready. Blue Note closes at 9pm, and although we have a 5-10 minute grace period there, you should be in the process of final scoring by 9, or else pack it up and play again another day.

Game night is open to all ages! Blue Note is a brewery, not a bar, so there's no restriction against minors. In addition to their crafted-in-house beer selection, water and sodas are available.

You're not required to buy anything in order to join us, but we do encourage you to support local businesses. Blue Note's chef team makes a mean pizza, and there are other nearby downtown restaurants and businesses.
source: Meetup Yolo County Board Game Gathering

title: Beginning Charcoal Drawing
description:
Pence Adult Art Programs
Learn the basics of charcoal drawing with Sophie Jorcino! In this one-day workshop, you will learn value shading, texture techniques, and sketching exercises, all while learning to use different charcoal materials that are included with the class fee. This workshop is open to everyone ages 16 and up- no art experience required.

Beginning Charcoal Drawing
Saturday\, June 27 \| 11:30 AM \- 3:30 PM
$75 Members, $95 Non-Members (All materials included)

**Registration is required through the [Pence's website (CLICK HERE)](https://pencegallery.org/education/programs/workshops-and-classes/).**

Any questions? Email pencegallery@gmail.com.

title: Blood on the Clocktower at Blue Note
description:
Yolo County Board Game Gathering
The first Wednesday of every month, we play _Blood on the Clocktower_, a social deduction game in the vein of Werewolf/Mafia/The Resistance, with a few twists!

We'll have a circle of chairs reserved inside Blue Note, near the back, with a Yolo County Board Game Gathering sign on the table. Just say hello, and we'll show you some games, pull some players together, and get to having fun!

We also usually have a few tables of players playing other board games, if social deduction isn't your bag!

Get a feel for Blood on the Clocktower here: https://youtu.be/4sfa8_kNxsQ
source: Meetup Yolo County Board Game Gathering

title: Free Yoga Practice
description:
Iama Embodied Community
🌀 **Move with intention. Connect with your body. Celebrate community.**

Join us every **Thursday at 6 PM** for an embodied yoga practice designed to help you release tension, ground yourself, and cultivate presence. This is a trauma-informed, all-levels class, no prior yoga experience needed. Expect a Hatha-Vinyasa flow, breathwork, and a space to listen to your body’s wisdom.

🌿 **Every last Thursday of the month, stay after class for a community potluck dinner!**

📍 **Location**: 727 T St. Sacramento CA 95811
✨ **All our events are free. Donations are welcome.**
📩 **Questions? Reach out at** embody.iama@gmail.com
🔗 **Follow us on Instagram:** [@embody.iama](https://www.instagram.com/embody.iama)

title: Conscious Conversations & Somatic Practices
description:
Iama Embodied Community
This is a spiritual community that honors the body’s wisdom and lived experience. Whether you’re healing from religious trauma, deconstructing beliefs, exploring spirituality, or just want to be with other conscious persons, this space is for you.

Expect conscious conversations, meditation, breathwork, sound healing, and other somatic practices to help reconnect with ourselves and each other.

we share a potluck dinner every last Monday of the month.

📅 **Date & Time**: Mondays 6-8 PM
📍 **Location**: 727 T St. Sacramento, Ca, 95811
💜 **All our events are free, donations supported.**
✨ **No prior experience or spiritual beliefs necessary.**
📩 **RSVP/ Questions? Reach out at** embody.iama@gmail.com
🔗 **Follow us on Instagram:** [@embody.iama](https://www.instagram.com/embody.iama)

PS: we meet in the social hall of Parkview Presbyterian Church. Please find us through the back entrance, the brown gate on Solons alley. You may park in the back, move the orange post to park and please return it when you leave.

title: Gentle yoga
description:
Iama Embodied Community
This is a slow and mindful practice that emphasizes deep breathing, body awareness, and nervous system regulation. This class is accessible to all abilities, providing a supportive and trauma-informed space to reconnect with the body.

Sessions alternate between floor yoga and chair yoga (on the 2nd and 4th Tuesday). Stay a bit longer on the last Tuesday of the month and join us for coffee or tea afterward!

Like all our events, this class is free, donations are welcome.
